CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-21-2015 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE JOHN K. STEWART; Clerk EBura; Proceedings not reported. (610)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-19-2015 Continued To Apr-21-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement And For Entry Of Judgment Ppursuant To Terms Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jan-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DOUGLAS N. AKAY of Akay Law, Tel No. (415) 764-1999 appearing for Plaintiff Patrick Brosnan, dba Brosnan Plumbing;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PATRICK BROSNAN, DBA BROSNAN PLUMBING'S Motion To Enforce Settlement And For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement Agreement is granted. Judgment shall be entered against Defendant in the amount of $46,763.41 pursuant to the terms of the parties' Settlement Agreement. The Settlement Agreement provides that default can be evidenced by "a Declaration of Counsel for [Plaintiff] certifying under penalty of perjury that an uncured default has occurred and the amount thereof, calculated under the provisions hereof." (Declaration of Douglas Akay, Ex. A, Settlement Agreement, par. 6.) The Settlement Agreement further provides for a 10% interest rate. (Id.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement And For Entry Of Judgment Ppursuant To Terms Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jan-09-2015 Continued To Jan-28-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PATRICK BROSNAN, DBA BROSNAN PLUMBING'S Motion To Enforce Settlement And For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement Agreement is continued to January 28, 2015 due to Plaintiff's counsel's declaration stating that he was never served with and has not seen the opposition. Defendant is ordered to immediately serve Plaintiff's counsel with the opposition papers. Plaintiff may file a reply brief up to five court days before the new hearing date. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Apr-08-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THOMAS COLL'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT. Appearances: Jason Honaker, Esq. specially appearing for Plaintiff, and opposing party; John F. Henning III, Law Offices of John F. Henning III, (415) 255-7675, appearing for Defendant Thomas Coll, dba Coll Construction, and moving party. Hearing held, the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted. Mr. Henning to remit one thousand dollars to plaintiff as reasonable attorney's fees for obtaining the default and default judgment. Defendant has five days to answer. Payment due date is thirty days from today's date, check made out jointly to Plaintiff and Plaintiff's counsel. CCP section 473(c)(1)(A).(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-09-2008 Continued To Oct-31-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Sep-10-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Judge Diane Elan Wick, Clerk Karissa Shaw, Reporter Candace Yount-CSR #2737. (611)

APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth MacDonald specially appearing on behalf of the plaintiff, Brosnan Plumbing.
No appearance by the defendant.

This matter comes on calendar for a Default Prove-Up Hearing.

Plaintiff's witness Lynn Martin is sworn and examined regarding the defendant's outstanding balance. The court reviews invoices provided by counsel.

The court needs the legal authorities cited by counsel in order to justify the pre-judgment interest requested. The witness does not have the authority to waive the pre-judgment interest claim made by Brosnan Plumbing.

Plaintiff's counsel will provide a memorandum to the court with the citations for pre-judgment interest as soon as possible. The court will not take the matter under submission until the memo is submitted.

9/5/08 - Points & Authorities in Support of Interest Award filed by the plaintiff and submitted to the court. Matter is taken under submission.

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Aug-14-2008 Set For Trial On Aug-14-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 611

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Aug-14-2008 Continued To Aug-21-08 At 03:45 P.M. In Dept 611
MINI MINUTES: Judge Diane Elan Wick, Clerk Karissa Shaw, Reporter Candace Yount CSR #2737. (611)

APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth MacDonald specially appearing for plaintiff Brosnan Plumbing.
No appearances by the defendant or any defense counsel.

This matter comes on calendar for a Default Prove-Up Hearing assigned by Master Calendar. Due to witness scheduling issues and miscommunication issues amongst counsel, Ms. MacDonald requests a 1 week continuance of this matter. The court grants the request and continues the matter to August 21, 2008 at 3:45 p.m. in Department 611.

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
